steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
The expression subtracts the entire second polynomial, so the minus sign changes the sign of every term inside the second set of parentheses. This turns $-4x^2$ into $+4x^2$, $+3x$ into $-3x$, and $-9$ into $+9$:
$$(3x^2-5)-(-4x^2+3x-9)=3x^2-5+4x^2-3x+9$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: Now group matching terms: the $x^2$ terms combine with each other, the only $x$ term stays $-3x$, and the constants $-5$ and $9$ combine to make $4$. The simplified expression is: $$3x^2+4x^2-3x+(-5+9)=7x^2-3x+4$$ The answer is A.
